title: ajuga chamaepitys var. glabra
intro: Ajuga chamaepitys var. glabra, also known as Ajuga glabra or Bugle, is a plant species in the mint family, Lamiaceae, characterized by its small, blue or white flowers and hairy leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic activities. The health benefits of Ajuga chamaepitys var. glabra include treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, as well as relieving pain and reducing inflammation. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
